↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 🇱🇧 The United States has imposed sanctions on an allegedly extensive illicit oil smuggling network operated allegedly by senior Iranian elites, targeting individuals, companies and vessels that allegedly generate revenue for Tehran and its proxies. The designations include Chetan Prakash Balhotra, an Indian national based in the UAE, and Seyed Naiemaei Badroddin Moosavi, a allegedly Hezbollah‑linked financier operating from the UK, UAE and Turkey. OFAC also blacklisted more than a dozen firms and shipping entities, including ACS Global BV in the Netherlands, ACS Trading LLC and Lotus Universal LLC in Dubai, and several Marshall Islands‑registered shipping companies such as Anika Lines Inc., Aura Lines Inc. and Nardie International S.A. Eight oil tankers – including the Anaya, Anika, Aura, Bellaris, Cauveri, Daphne V, Horae, Silvar and Versa – were also designated for their role in allegedly transporting Iranian oil. The action targets Iran’s oil export revenues that fund its military programmes and support for Hezbollah. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇺🇸🇮🇷The U.S. Treasury announced that they’re aggressively moving forward with Economic Fury. They advised financial institutions that they will be leveraging the full range of tools they have to deploy secondary sanctions against financial institutions…" ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 🇱🇧 The U.S. Treasury has sanctioned Mohammad Hossein Shamkhani, on of former senior Iranian official Ali Shamkhani, targeting Iran’s oil transportation network as the first sanction under Economic Fury. The U.S. Treasury said it sanctioned more than two dozen individuals, companies, and vessels involved in what it described as a large-scale network moving Iranian oil and generating billions of dollars in revenue for Tehran and its allies. The network uses front companies across multiple countries to manage shipping, logistics, and financial operations while masking its activities. Several vessels accused of transporting Iranian and Russian petroleum products were also designated as blocked property. The Treasury added that the measures also target a financial network allegedly involved in exchanging Iranian oil for Venezuelan gold, which it said helped fund groups including Hezbollah and Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps. @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇺🇸🇮🇷🇷🇺U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessent confirmed that the U.S. will not renew waivers on Iranian and Russia oil. "We will not be renewing the general license on Russian oil, and we will not be renewing the general license on Iranian oil. Because, again…" ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 U.S. Press Secretary Leavitt's response to a question on the timeline for the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz: "Sure. I will never set timelines on behalf of the president of The United States. But with respect to the blockade, as you know, it has been fully implemented, and it's being enforced against vessels of all nations entering or departing Iranian ports. I wanna make that point clear. I've seen some misreporting on that as well. This includes all Iranian ports on the Arabian Gulf and the Gulf Of Oman. And our US forces in the region are supporting the freedom of navigation for vessels that are transiting the strait to and from non Iranian ports. So I know some in the press were confused about that. We are supporting the freedom of navigation, just not with respect to any tanker or vessel that would benefit the economy, of Iran as these negotiations continue." @wfwitness

↩️ Reply to OSINT Visioner RT @NSTRIKE1231 : 🇪🇺 🇺🇦 The European Union will allocate €1.07 billion under the European Defence Fund to support 57 new projects, some of which will include Ukrainian companies, according to EP. ▪️ The projects cover key defense areas such as artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, unmanned systems, and counter-drone technologies. ▪️ A total of 634 companies from 26 EU member states and Norway are involved. ▪️ With support from the EU Defence Innovation Office in Kyiv, Ukrainian partners have been integrated into selected projects. ▪️ One of the flagship initiatives is the STRATUS project, aimed at developing an AI-based cybersecurity system to counter drone swarms, with participation from a Ukrainian subcontractor using real battlefield experience. ▪️ Companies can receive funding of up to €60,000 under these programs, which are also open to Ukrainian participants. ▪️ Overall, €675 million will go toward capability development across 32 projects, while €332 million will fund 25 research initiatives. Grant agreements are expected to be signed by the end of 2026. See the latest updates with us: @NSTRIKE1231 tweet

↩️ Reply to OSINT Visioner 🇪🇺 🇺🇦 BIG | The European Union is allocating 1.07 billion euros for the implementation of 57 new projects within the framework of the European Defence Fund. Ukrainian companies will also participate in some of them, reports EP. The projects cover critically important defense areas, including artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, unmanned systems, and counter-drone measures. A total of 634 companies from 26 EU member states and Norway are involved in the initiatives. With the support of the EU Defence Innovation Office in Kyiv, it became possible to integrate Ukrainian partners into individual projects. One of the main initiatives is the STRATUS project, which involves creating an AI-based cybersecurity system against drone swarms. It includes a Ukrainian subcontractor, allowing the use of practical combat experience. Within these programs, companies can receive funding of up to 60,000 euros. Ukrainian companies also have the opportunity to participate in the programs. A total of 675 million euros is allocated for the development of defense capabilities for 32 projects, and another 332 million euros for 25 research initiatives. Grant agreements are planned to be signed by the end of 2026. See the latest updates with us: @visionergeo tweet

🔄 Forwarded from Rybar in English: 📝 ZNPP Back on Diesel 📝 Ukrainian forces continue attempting to stage a provocation at the Zaporizhia Nuclear Power Plant . This week, the station experienced another blackout — the 14th since the start of the SMO. The cause was an AFU strike on areas near the station. The attack damaged the last high-voltage line "Ferrosplavnaya-1" , forcing the NPP to switch to diesel generators. This is not the first shelling that has led to such an outcome. 🔻 What Else Was Hit and the Consequences? ▪️ Shellings often target the areas around the Zaporizhia NPP, including Energodar. Direct hits on the station itself have not been recorded, though this does not rule out consequences. ▪️ Shellings frequently damage the two supply lines — the backup "Ferrosplavnaya-1" and the main "Dniprova." The latter remains non-operational, meaning the station currently has no external power supply and operates on generators. ▪️ "Ferrosplavnaya-1" has been shut down three times since the beginning of the year. In the first two cases, repair work began after some time, with a local ceasefire declared. ▪️ The IAEA has not changed its position, constantly calling on both sides to account for risks and organizing local ceasefires for supply line repairs. 📌 The situation remains difficult. It's unknown when the "Ferrosplavnaya-1" line will be restored or how long the station will operate on generators. Repairs require a local ceasefire , which takes time. ❗️ The intensity of AFU strikes on the areas around the NPP, damaging the supply lines, is not decreasing and sometimes even increasing . This is despite ongoing talks on restoring the "Dniprova" line. #Russia #Ukraine ✈ RU | ✈ EN | ✉ MAX ✉️ VK | ✉️ RuTube | ✉️

President Trump said this morning that he thinks we're nearing the end. The US kept their side on the ceasefire. We've stopped firing. The Straits Of Hormuz have not been completely reopened. It's bank week here in DC, so I've been meeting with a lot of my Middle Eastern counterparts, the finance ministers, and they all say that once the straits are open, they can start pumping again within one week." @wfwitness
